comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: AC1 refined to match the delivered+user-approved outcome: active/reference docs carry zero live-config references to removed keys; changelog/migration removal-RECORDS (release-notes.md, migrations.md) + historical ADRs are retained (they announce the removal, as 7ed9's AC required). Confirmed by grep: only remaining active-doc hits are past-tense 'removed/one-way-door' records in migrations.md + release-notes.md.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Removing config keys leaves stale references across docs, CLI help text, and release
notes. This story is the accuracy sweep: delete/correct every reference to a removed
key and verify the config docs match the post-prune schema. It should close LAST
(after the removal stories land) so it sweeps the final state.

## What

Sweep and correct references to each REMOVED key:
- `require_signature_for_close`: `docs/manifest-signing.md:60`, `docs/config.md:96,386`,
  `docs/llm-framework.md:471`, `docs/migrations.md:142`, `docs/release-notes.md:46`,
  `src/rebar/_cli/help/transition.txt:7`, `docs/cli-reference.md:377`.
- `baseline_dual_write` / `baseline_consumer_swap`: `docs/jira-sync-setup.md:329-348`
  (the phased-rollout section), `docs/config.md`, any ADR-0026 operator notes.
- `progressive_drift_refresh` / `remediation_mode` / `novelty_drop_active`:
  `docs/config.md`, `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, `docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md` if referenced.
- Then a full-repo grep for each removed key name across `docs/`, `*.md`, `src/rebar/_cli/help/`.

Also: correct any doc statements that become inaccurate once behavior is hardcoded
(e.g. "default on; explicit false is the back-out" must no longer imply a toggle),
and record the KEEP decisions (signature mirror, completion floor) where docs discuss them.

## Scope

Docs/help/release-notes only — no behavior change. Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, and
the require_signature retirement having landed so the final state is swept.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No active/reference documentation presents any removed key (`require_signature_for_close`, `baseline_dual_write`, `baseline_consumer_swap`, `progressive_drift_refresh`, `remediation_mode`, `novelty_drop_active`, `emit_legacy_signature_mirror`) as a live, configurable option. Guide/reference docs (`docs/config.md`, `plan-review-gate.md`, `review-kernel.md`, `manifest-signing.md`, `llm-framework.md`, `jira-sync-setup.md`, `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`) and CLI help contain zero such references. Retained, by explicit decision: past-tense removal/one-way-door RECORDS in the changelog/migration docs (`docs/release-notes.md`, `docs/migrations.md`) and historical ADRs — these announce the removal (as 7ed9's AC required) and are the appropriate historical home, analogous to tickets/ADRs.
- [ ] Docs describing the now-hardcoded behaviors no longer describe a config toggle; the retained tuning params are still documented.
- [ ] `docs/config.md` matches the pruned schema exactly (every listed key exists; no removed key listed).
- [ ] CLI help (`transition` etc.) and golden help fixtures regenerated and consistent.
compiled_state.title: Doc-accuracy sweep: purge removed config keys, correct config docs
